Geranium (Geranium spp.) is a plant species in the family Geraniaceae, characterized by its showy flowers and distinctive foliage. The health benefits of Geranium include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Geranium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been found to possess various pharmacological activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Geranium include tinctures, teas, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments used for skin care and wound healing.
